title: Fixed SKGLView on Android not being rendered when using a TabBar
body:
**Description of Change**

Currently an `SKGLView` on Android will not render when it appears for a second time, such as when the `SKGLView` is placed on a MAUI Tab Page in a TabBar. The first time the Tab with the `SKGLView` is displayed it correctly renders, however when switching to a different Tab and then coming back, the `SKGLView` will not render anymore.

This PR solves this issue by constructing the internal `GLThread` with the current size of the View, instead of relying on Android to call `GLTextureView.OnLayoutChange()`. If the size of the View has not been set yet by Android (which happens when the `SKGLView` is displayed for the first time), the GLTextureView's Width and Height are both 0, just as the default of the GLThread used to be, so it is safe to use these values when constructing the `GLThread`.

**Bugs Fixed**

- Fixes #2550
- Most probably also fixes #2445
- Possibly also fixes Mapsui/Mapsui#1896 which has already been mitigated by Mapsui/Mapsui#1934

**API Changes**

No public API changes.

Private API Change:

- `SkiaSharp.Views.Android.GLTextureView.GLThread(WeakReference<GLTextureView> glTextureViewWeakRef)` => `GLThread(WeakReference<GLTextureView> glTextureViewWeakRef, int initialWidth, int initialHeight)`

**Behavioral Changes**

None.

**Required skia PR**

None.

**PR Checklist**

- [ ] Has tests (if omitted, state reason in description)
- [ ] Rebased on top of main at time of PR
- [ ] Merged related skia PRs
- [ ] Changes adhere to coding standard
- [ ] Updated documentation

comments.author: SimonvBez
comments.body:
I just came across #2445 and added it to the PR description, as I suspect this PR will solve that as well.
The lack of redrawing is probably not exclusive to TabBar pages only, but applies to Shell Pages in general (since those pages will be reused, compared to stack-based navigation pages which are usually constructed and destructed on demand)

Please let me know if I need to do anything like rebasing on top of the latest main, provide more details or conduct more tests 😃

comments.author: janusw
comments.body: Issue #2550 is growing into a real blocker for me with recent MAUI versions (> 9.0.51). Also this fix has been lingering here for quite a while. @mattleibow Would you mind to consider it for merging?

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
## ✅ Review: Ready to Merge

**PR #3076 -- Fixed SKGLView on Android not being rendered when using a TabBar**

5 independent AI model reviews (Claude Opus, Claude Sonnet, Gemini Pro, GPT-5.3 Codex) unanimously found **no bugs, race conditions, or correctness issues**. The fix is minimal, correct, and directly addresses the rendering regression.

**CI Status:** CLA ✅ passed | Azure DevOps build ⚠️ pending at review time -- no PR-specific failures observed

**Recommendation: ✅ Approve**

---

<details>
<summary>📋 Detailed Analysis (click to expand)</summary>

### What the Fix Does

The `GLThread` constructor now receives the current view's `Width`/`Height` so that when a tab is re-visited (view re-attached with unchanged dimensions), the render thread starts with non-zero initial dimensions and `ReadyToDraw()` unblocks immediately -- without waiting for an `OnLayoutChange` that never fires because the size hasn't changed.

### Scenario Analysis

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| **Tab re-visited with same-sized view (the bug case)** | ✅ Fixed: `Width`/`Height` > 0 passed in, `ReadyToDraw()` unblocked |
| **`SetRenderer` called before layout (`Width=0, Height=0`)** | ✅ No regression: identical behavior to before the fix |
| **`surfaceSizeChanged = true` with non-zero initial size** | ✅ Correct: `OnSurfaceChanged` called with proper dimensions on first render |
| **Thread safety of initial `width`/`height` values** | ✅ Safe: fields set before `glThread.Start()`, happens-before guaranteed |
| **Double-resize if `OnLayoutChange` also fires** | ✅ Harmless: redundant pulse, at most one extra `OnSurfaceChanged` with correct dims |

### Thread Safety Deep-Dive

- The constructor sets `width`/`height` fields **before** `glThread.Start()` is called, establishing a happens-before relationship per the .NET memory model
- All subsequent writes to `width`/`height` (via `OnWindowResize`) are protected by `lock(threadManager)`, the same lock the GL thread holds when reading them
- `OnDetachedFromWindow` calls `RequestExitAndWait()` which blocks until the old thread exits -- no overlap between old and new `GLThread` instances

### First Render Path Analysis

On first draw, the GL thread hits both:
2. The `surfaceSizeChanged` branch (~line 940): captures `w = width, h = height`

These two branches are additive, not conflicting. `OnSurfaceChanged` receives the correct non-zero dimensions on first render.

### Test Coverage

No new tests added (PR checklist confirms this). This is acceptable -- the fix addresses an Android-specific rendering lifecycle issue involving tab switching that would require an instrumented MAUI app on a real device to test. The change is a 2-line constructor parameter addition with straightforward logic.

### Consensus

All 5 review models independently returned **no findings**. Zero issues flagged across all severity levels (Critical, Moderate, Minor).

</details>

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
Device test added in #3554 that **proves this fix works**.

- **Without this fix**: test fails with `got 0x0` (GLThread starts with zero dimensions)
- **With this fix**: test passes ✔ (~1600ms)

The test uses native Android `RemoveView()`/`AddView()` + reflection to read `GLThread.width`/`height` immediately after `OnAttachedToWindow()` fires — before any async `OnLayoutChange` callback can mask the bug. This is the exact window that causes the real tab-bar rendering failure.
